finding_results.notes: Rewrote two Mallick questions to meander through concrete house-detail before landing the investigative point. At line 101, Mallick now approaches the relationship question through the draft board ('I keep looking at that board in there... the little red circles... The commissioner, is that right?') before asking how Doug knew Martin. At line 143, she routes through the sliding door ('Does it always rattle like that? I keep thinking somebody's coming in') before pivoting to the Friday night timeline. Doug's monosyllabic answers still respond to the buried asks ('College. Same group.' and 'Midnight. Maybe later.'). Removed the explanatory narration at line 171 that previously told the reader her questions were 'meandering and wrapped in house talk' — the dialogue now carries that method without narrator certification. Also restored fourteen contraction regressions from the snapshot throughout Doug's close-third narration (e.g. 'had not' back to 'hadn't', 'was not' to 'wasn't', 'did not' to 'didn't') to match Doug's high contraction level per the style bible and preserve the snapshot's spoken texture.

summary: Fixed Mallick's deck interview by rewriting two questions to arrive through domestic tangent (draft board, sliding door) rather than direct Q-and-A, and removed the explanatory narration that was telling the reader about her meandering method instead of letting the dialogue demonstrate it. Restored contraction level across Doug's close-third narration to match the snapshot's established spoken texture and his high contraction profile.
